embeds.title: 🧠 Dreams & Nightmares
embeds.description:
Approaching the vortex through the thickest of the mist, you find your mind assaulted on all sides by the gloom's strange influence. Resisting it proves difficult, and your mental defenses are soon overwhelmed. In a moment, the battle raging around you falls away, muffled and muted by the mind-altering effects of the mists. Your ears ring, drowning out shouts of orders, the blast of spells and the clang of steel. Your skin tingles as some of your energy and vitality is drained from you (no mechanical effect, yet). The smell of smoke from fire and ozone from lightning fade to nothing. You can feel yourself begin to slow, and the world begins to turn over itself, with no clear indication of up and down. Your vision blurs as a dark haze creeps across your eyes.

... and then you find yourself someplace else. The haze takes on a faint warm glow, carrying a certain warmth and familiarity that seem out of place given your surroundings a moment ago. Through the haze, you can just make out the shape of two young elven women. Each blonde, exquisitely beautiful, and nearly indistinguishable from one another. Somewhere deep in your muddled thoughts, you are certain they look familiar to you.

The haze flickers, like light catching in a mirror, and the scene changes. The two women stand, dressed in regal fineries, in the throne room amidst a courtly gathering. The two speak with a man in deep purple robes bearing the insignia of the Arcanum Guild. Laughing. Flirting. He blushes, smitten.

Another flicker, another change; this time one of the twins speaks with him alone, in a corner of the throne room, away from prying ears.

Another change, faster than the first, and the mage's hands are a blur as he weaves a spell. The hazy image carries no sound, but by the looks of it, it is powerful. The target - the other twin. The gloom - looking much the same as the gloom through which you now fight - swirls about her, sealing her in, and then ... 
all goes dark.

As the flashes of the vision fade to darkness, your senses clear. Once again on the battlefield, still standing, with only a slight stagger to mark your moment of disorientation. As your senses return to you, it dawns on you why you recognized the sisters. She was younger in the vision, but the woman who spoke to the mage, just prior to the spell he cast upon her sister, was unmistakable to any citizen of Ost in Edhil.

Queen Sel'ani.
